title: COVID-19 Vaccination Responses with Different Vaccine Platforms in Patients with Inborn Errors of Immunity.
ehr_evidence: The study used laboratory assays (ELISA, ELISPOT, and flow cytometry) on a clinical cohort rather than electronic health records.
summary: The study evaluated the safety and immunogenicity of various COVID-19 vaccines (Sputnik, AstraZeneca, Sinopharm, Moderna, and Pfizer) in patients with inborn errors of immunity (IEI) in Argentina.
key_findings: No moderate/severe vaccine-associated adverse events were observed.
Anti-S/RBD titers and neutralizing antibodies were significantly lower in IEI patients compared to age-matched healthy controls.
Moderna vaccines elicited a stronger humoral response in the pediatric population.
84.5% of IEI patients showed a positive S-specific IFN-γ response.
captured_features: S-specific circulating T follicular-helper cells (TFH) frequency
population: 118 adults and pediatric patients with inborn errors of immunity (IEI) and 37 age-matched healthy controls in Argentina
